# PRP: Speculative Weights Auto-Calibration

**Feature**: Replace the "email Claude a copy-paste prompt" speculative-preset weight calibration loop with a data-driven pipeline that runs in the app itself — proposals generated by an algorithm, one-click apply from email, full DB history.
**Feature Branch**: `feature/speculative-weights-auto-calibration`
**Created**: 2026-04-22
**One-Pass Confidence Score**: 8/10

> **Status (2026-04-23)**: Phase 1 (proposal mode) **shipped** in v2.166.0, with polish in v2.166.1 (security audit fixes), v2.166.2 (hygiene: ProposalStatus class + shared TTL constant), and v2.166.3 (test-coverage gaps). Phase 2 (auto-apply + auto-revert + rolling windows) remains deferred — it should be a **separate PRP** once enough real proposals have been generated through Phase 1 to validate the algorithm's suggestions. No real user has crossed the 500-closed-position threshold yet (feature shipped hours ago), so Phase 2 is data-blocked, not code-blocked.

> **8/10** because the infrastructure (monitor, email, tokens, signed-URL endpoints, bucket-service tests, migration template, domain schemas) is all in place from v2.165.0–v2.165.4 and this PRP layers a clean, mechanically testable algorithm on top. The only real risk is the normalization-with-clamps math — that's where the unit-test blueprint below is most detailed. No LLM tuning, no schema gymnastics, no new infra.

---

## TL;DR

A background monitor already watches closed speculative positions and emails the user a calibration alert at ≥50 closed trades + ≥20pp component divergence. This PRP extends that monitor so that when the sample size also crosses ≥500, it computes a **proposed new WEIGHTS dict** via a proportional-to-alpha algorithm, persists it as a pending row in a new `trading.speculative_weights_proposals` table, and appends a one-click "Apply proposed weights" signed-token link to the existing email next to the existing Claude prompt block. A POST endpoint behind that token marks the proposal `applied`, invalidates an in-process cache, and the scorer picks up the new per-user weights on the next AI evaluation. The Claude copy-paste escape hatch stays for nuanced cases.

Phase 2 (auto-apply + auto-revert + rolling windows) is explicitly deferred — this PRP ships proposal mode only.

---

## Decisions on the 5 Open Questions

| # | Question | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Per-user vs global weights | **Per-user** | Autonomy (one user's bad luck doesn't contaminate others). Scorer already has `user_id` + `db` in scope. Cost is a cached DB read per evaluation, not per candle. |
| 2 | Weight persistence | **Latest `status='applied'` row in `speculative_weights_proposals` + in-process TTL cache (60s + explicit invalidation on apply)** | No separate "current" table needed; history IS the source of truth. TTL handles multi-worker staleness without pub/sub. |
| 3 | Schema — current table vs history-only | **Single `speculative_weights_proposals` table with status field. No separate current/history tables.** | "Current" = most recent applied row per user (one indexed query). One table is simpler and the state machine (pending → applied / rejected / superseded / reverted) lives in one place. |
| 4 | Clamp vs normalize precedence | **Clamp-first then iteratively normalize.** | Floor/ceiling are trading-safety invariants; sum-to-100 is a range-contract convention. If the two conflict, pin the binding constraint and redistribute remainder among the free components. If impossible, raise and do NOT generate a proposal that cycle. |
| 5 | Auto-revert override UX | **Deferred to Phase 2.** Phase 1 has no auto-revert. | Phase 2 will include a "Keep new weights anyway" signed-URL that re-applies the reverted weights as a fresh proposal. |

---

## Context & Goal

### User Story

> "The v2.165.x speculative preset email tells me to 'open Claude and paste this prompt' once there's enough data to recalibrate. That works, but it's clumsy — I'd rather the system just tell me 'based on 637 closed trades, here's what the numbers say your new weights should be, click to apply' and only fall back to the Claude prompt when I want a second opinion. Store everything so I can audit later."

### Problem

`speculative_calibration_monitor` currently emails a copy-paste prompt when ≥50 closed positions + ≥20pp component divergence accumulate. The user then has to open Claude Code, paste the block, let Claude re-derive the stats, propose weight changes, edit `speculative_signals.py`, run tests, and ship. This is five manual steps where the first four could be deterministic code.

The scorer's weight source is a module-level constant (`speculative_signals.WEIGHTS`) — there's no per-user customization path, no history of changes, and no way to roll back a bad tune.

### Goal

Ship Phase 1 (proposal mode):

1. **Every time the existing alert fires AND sample size ≥500**, also compute a proposal via a deterministic algorithm and persist it as a pending row.
2. **Email body** gets a new "AUTOMATED PROPOSAL" section next to the existing Claude prompt block, showing current→proposed weight deltas and a signed one-click "Apply" URL.
3. **POST /api/accounts/{id}/speculative-weights/apply-proposal** accepts the signed token, transitions the proposal to `applied`, invalidates the per-user weights cache.
4. **Scorer resolves weights per-user** via a new `get_effective_weights(db, user_id)` helper — falls back to module defaults when the user has never applied a proposal.
5. **Frontend (minimal)**: a history block under the Speculative Bucket Settings card showing past proposals + status, plus toast handler reuse for apply confirmations. No dedicated new page.

### Non-Goals

- **Auto-apply without user confirmation** (Phase 2).
- **Auto-revert on post-change underperformance** (Phase 2).
- **Rolling-window analysis for concept drift** (Phase 2).
- **Tuning the scorer's component functions themselves** (only the weight values change).
- **Tuning the LLM prompt or model choice.**
- **Per-bot weights** (per-user is the finest granularity).
- **Admin-can-tune-another-user's-weights** (each user applies only their own).

---

## Constraints

### Per-User Scope

All DB queries touching `speculative_weights_proposals` MUST filter by `user_id`. Cache keys are `user_id`-indexed. The apply-endpoint's signed token embeds both `sub` (user_id) and `proposal_id`; the endpoint must verify that the caller (`current_user.id`) matches `sub` before loading the proposal. Mirrors the v2.165.1 dismiss-endpoint pattern exactly.

### Backwards Compatibility

Existing users with no applied proposal MUST continue to see exactly the current module-default weights. The refactor of `score_speculative_setup()` to accept an optional `weights` parameter MUST default to `DEFAULT_WEIGHTS` (renamed from `WEIGHTS` to signal that it's a default, not the live value).

### Email Must Not Regress

The existing verbatim Claude copy-paste block MUST stay present in the email body regardless of whether a proposal was generated. The "COPY EVERYTHING BELOW/ABOVE THIS LINE" markers are preserved. The proposal section is additive, inserted between the component-performance table and the "TO ACT ON THIS" Claude section.

### PostgreSQL + SQLite Dual-Mode

Migration 084 must work on both backends (see `backend/migrations/db_utils.py`). All ForeignKey strings MUST be schema-qualified (e.g. `trading.speculative_weights_proposals.id`) — see MEMORY.md §Database gotchas.

### Idempotency & Safety

- Migration 084 is idempotent (safe to re-run).
- Apply endpoint is idempotent for the SAME `(proposal_id, user_id)` — second apply returns 409 "already applied" without error-spamming the log.
- Cache invalidation is best-effort; the 60-second TTL is the safety net.

### Calibration Monitor Must Stay Alive

Proposal creation runs INSIDE `_check_user()` but wrapped in its own try/except so a proposal failure (e.g. algorithm raises on impossible constraints, or DB error) does NOT block the email + toast from firing. Phase 1 behavior when proposal generation fails: email sends with no auto-proposal section (the Claude prompt block is still present), log the exception.

---

## Existing Patterns (Reference)

### Calibration Monitor — `backend/app/services/speculative_calibration_monitor.py`

```python
async def _check_user(db, user_id: int) -> None:
    account = await _account_for_speculative_user(db, user_id)
    if account is None:
        return
    last_alerted = account.speculative_calibration_last_alerted_at
    if last_alerted is not None and (datetime.utcnow() - last_alerted) < timedelta(days=COOLDOWN_DAYS):
        return
    analysis = await analyze_speculative_calibration(db, user_id)
    if analysis is None:
        return
    # ... send email, broadcast toast, stamp cooldown ...
```

**Extension point**: after `analysis is None` check, add a `proposal = await _maybe_create_proposal(db, user_id, account.id, analysis)` call. Then compute `apply_url` via `build_apply_proposal_url()` and thread `proposal`, `apply_url` into the email send call.

### Dismiss-Token Pattern — `backend/app/services/speculative_calibration_token.py`

The apply-token module mirrors this file exactly. Separate module (not extending this one) so the type-check boundary between `speculative_calibration_dismiss` and `speculative_calibration_apply_proposal` stays crisp.

### Migration Template — `backend/migrations/083_ai_opinion_log_speculative.py`

PG + SQLite dual branches via `is_postgres()`. Migration 084 follows exactly this pattern. JSONB on PG, TEXT on SQLite for the weights columns.

### Calibration Analysis Output Shape — `backend/app/services/speculative_bucket_service.py::analyze_speculative_calibration`

Already returns:
```python
{
    "total_closed": 67,
    "wins": 9, "losses": 58,
    "overall_win_rate_pct": 13.4,
    "overall_realized_pnl_usd": -127.40,
    "components": [
        {"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 52, "win_rate_pct": 19.2},
        # ... one per component that fired at least once ...
    ],
    "top_component": "volume_surge", "top_win_rate_pct": 19.2,
    "bottom_component": "correlation_break", "bottom_win_rate_pct": 4.0,
    "divergence_pp": 15.2,
}
```

The tuner consumes this dict directly — no new aggregation query needed.

### Signed-URL Endpoint — `POST /api/accounts/{id}/speculative-calibration/dismiss`

Owner-only (`Account.user_id == current_user.id`, returns 404 for non-owners), validates the signed token's `sub == current_user.id` and `account_id == path.account_id`, returns 403 on any token mismatch. Apply-endpoint clones this pattern and ADDS a `proposal_id` match + a status-must-be-pending check.

---

## Recommended Design

### 1. Data Model

New table `trading.speculative_weights_proposals`:

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `SERIAL PRIMARY KEY` (PG) / `INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT` (SQLite) | |
| `user_id` | `INTEGER NOT NULL FK auth.users.id` | Indexed, scopes every query |
| `account_id` | `INTEGER NULL FK trading.accounts.id` | Which account triggered the alert (user may have several) |
| `status` | `VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'` | `pending` → `applied` / `rejected` / `superseded` / `reverted` |
| `algorithm` | `VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL` | e.g. `'proportional-alpha-v1'` |
| `sample_size` | `INTEGER NOT NULL` | Closed positions the proposal is based on |
| `overall_win_rate_pct` | `DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL` | For display + later analysis |
| `baseline_weights` | `JSONB NOT NULL` (PG) / `TEXT` (SQLite) | What weights were active at time of proposal |
| `proposed_weights` | `JSONB NOT NULL` (PG) / `TEXT` (SQLite) | What the algorithm suggests |
| `divergence_pp` | `DOUBLE PRECISION` | top-bottom pp gap — audit aid |
| `reason` | `TEXT` | Free-text explanation / algorithm-produced summary |
| `created_at` | `TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` | |
| `decided_at` | `TIMESTAMP NULL` | When user applied/rejected |
| `decided_by` | `INTEGER NULL FK auth.users.id` | Usually same as `user_id` |
| `reverted_by_proposal_id` | `INTEGER NULL FK self` | Phase 2 reserved; Phase 1 leaves null |

Indexes:
- `(user_id, status)` — lists pending proposals, finds current applied quickly
- `(user_id, decided_at DESC) WHERE status='applied'` (partial — PG only) — "current weights for user X" lookup

State machine (Phase 1 states only):
```
                 ┌──────────┐
 [create]───────▶│ pending  │
                 └────┬─────┘
                      │
            ┌─────────┼─────────┐
            ▼         ▼         ▼
       applied   rejected   superseded
       (user    (explicit  (newer pending
       clicked  decline,   arrived before
       Apply)   Phase 2+)  user acted on
                           this one)
```

### 2. Algorithm — `propose_weights()`

Pure function, stateless, no I/O. Same file as scorer constants or a new `app/services/speculative_weights_tuner.py`. (Recommendation: **new file** to keep `speculative_signals.py` strictly about scoring logic.)

```python
LEARNING_RATE = 0.3               # k
MAX_WEIGHT_CHANGE_PER_CYCLE = 5   # ±5 points from baseline per proposal
WEIGHT_FLOOR = 5
WEIGHT_CEILING = 40
PROPOSAL_MIN_SAMPLE_SIZE = 500
ALGORITHM_NAME = "proportional-alpha-v1"


def propose_weights(
    current_weights: Dict[str, int],
    component_stats: List[Dict],           # from analyze_speculative_calibration()["components"]
    overall_win_rate_pct: float,
    *,
    learning_rate: float = LEARNING_RATE,
    max_change: float = MAX_WEIGHT_CHANGE_PER_CYCLE,
    floor: float = WEIGHT_FLOOR,
    ceiling: float = WEIGHT_CEILING,
) -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Return new weights (integers summing to 100).

    Raises ValueError on impossible constraints (e.g. all components pinned
    at ceiling summing to >100). Caller treats as 'no proposal this cycle'.
    """
    # 1. Apply proportional-to-alpha delta per component.
    stats_by_name = {s["name"]: s for s in component_stats}
    raw = {}
    for name, old in current_weights.items():
        stat = stats_by_name.get(name)
        if stat is None or stat["fires"] == 0:
            # Component never fired → preserve its current weight untouched.
            raw[name] = float(old)
            continue
        alpha = (stat["win_rate_pct"] - overall_win_rate_pct) / 100.0  # fraction
        new_w = float(old) * (1.0 + learning_rate * alpha)
        # 2. Cap per-cycle change at ±max_change.
        new_w = max(float(old) - max_change, min(float(old) + max_change, new_w))
        raw[name] = new_w

    # 3. Clamp to [floor, ceiling].
    clamped = {k: max(floor, min(ceiling, v)) for k, v in raw.items()}

    # 4. Iteratively normalize: scale-to-100, pin any violators, redistribute.
    normalized = _normalize_to_sum(clamped, target=100.0, floor=floor, ceiling=ceiling)

    # 5. Integer-round while preserving sum=100.
    return _integer_round_preserving_sum(normalized, target=100)
```

Normalization algorithm (also pure):

```python
def _normalize_to_sum(
    weights: Dict[str, float], *, target: float, floor: float, ceiling: float,
) -> Dict[str, float]:
    """Rescale so sum(weights) == target, iteratively pinning any that
    hit floor/ceiling. Converges in O(N) iterations for N components.
    Raises ValueError if constraints are impossible."""
    w = dict(weights)
    pinned: set[str] = set()
    for _ in range(len(w) + 1):  # Guaranteed convergence in ≤N+1 passes.
        free = [k for k in w if k not in pinned]
        pinned_sum = sum(w[k] for k in pinned)
        remaining = target - pinned_sum
        if not free:
            if abs(remaining) > 1e-6:
                raise ValueError(f"Cannot reach target — all pinned, remaining={remaining}")
            return w
        free_sum = sum(w[k] for k in free)
        if free_sum <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Free sum zero — cannot rescale")
        scale = remaining / free_sum
        any_pinned_this_pass = False
        for k in free:
            scaled = w[k] * scale
            if scaled <= floor + 1e-9:
                w[k] = floor; pinned.add(k); any_pinned_this_pass = True
            elif scaled >= ceiling - 1e-9:
                w[k] = ceiling; pinned.add(k); any_pinned_this_pass = True
            else:
                w[k] = scaled
        if not any_pinned_this_pass:
            # All free components accepted the scale, we're done.
            return w
    # Shouldn't reach here — the loop bound guarantees convergence.
    raise ValueError("Normalization failed to converge")


def _integer_round_preserving_sum(w: Dict[str, float], *, target: int) -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Round floats to ints while guaranteeing sum == target. Distributes
    the ±1 rounding error to the component with the largest fractional part
    (largest-remainder method)."""
    ints = {k: int(round(v)) for k, v in w.items()}
    delta = target - sum(ints.values())
    if delta == 0:
        return ints
    # Sort by fractional part descending (most-rounded-down first) for positive
    # delta, ascending for negative.
    fracs = sorted(
        ((k, v - int(v)) for k, v in w.items()),
        key=lambda kv: kv[1],
        reverse=(delta > 0),
    )
    for k, _ in fracs[: abs(delta)]:
        ints[k] += 1 if delta > 0 else -1
    return ints
```

### 3. Weight Resolution + Cache

New module `app/services/speculative_weights_cache.py` (small, focused):

```python
import time
from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.indicators.speculative_signals import DEFAULT_WEIGHTS  # renamed from WEIGHTS

_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 60
_cache: dict[int, tuple[dict, float]] = {}


async def get_effective_weights(db: AsyncSession, user_id: int) -> dict:
    """Return the currently-effective WEIGHTS dict for a user.

    Resolution order:
    1. In-process cache, if not expired.
    2. Latest `status='applied'` row in speculative_weights_proposals for user_id.
    3. Module defaults (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS).
    """
    now = time.monotonic()
    cached = _cache.get(user_id)
    if cached and cached[1] > now:
        return dict(cached[0])  # defensive copy

    from app.models import SpeculativeWeightsProposal
    stmt = (
        select(SpeculativeWeightsProposal.proposed_weights)
        .where(
            SpeculativeWeightsProposal.user_id == user_id,
            SpeculativeWeightsProposal.status == "applied",
        )
        .order_by(SpeculativeWeightsProposal.decided_at.desc())
        .limit(1)
    )
    row = (await db.execute(stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()
    weights = _parse_weights(row) if row is not None else dict(DEFAULT_WEIGHTS)
    _cache[user_id] = (weights, now + _CACHE_TTL_SECONDS)
    return dict(weights)


def invalidate_weights_cache(user_id: int | None = None) -> None:
    """Drop the cached entry so the next get_effective_weights() re-queries.
    Pass None to clear all entries (used for testing)."""
    if user_id is None:
        _cache.clear()
    else:
        _cache.pop(user_id, None)


def _parse_weights(raw) -> dict:
    """Normalize JSON-col output. PG JSONB returns dict directly; SQLite TEXT returns str."""
    if isinstance(raw, str):
        import json
        return json.loads(raw)
    return dict(raw or {})
```

### 4. Scorer Refactor

```python
# speculative_signals.py
# Rename WEIGHTS → DEFAULT_WEIGHTS to signal it's a default, not the live value.
DEFAULT_WEIGHTS: Dict[str, int] = {
    "volume_surge":          25,
    # ... (values unchanged) ...
}

# Preserve the sum-to-100 invariant.
assert sum(DEFAULT_WEIGHTS.values()) == 100, "DEFAULT_WEIGHTS must sum to 100"

# Back-compat alias for any code still reading WEIGHTS (remove in a later version).
WEIGHTS = DEFAULT_WEIGHTS


def score_speculative_setup(
    metrics: Dict[str, Any],
    btc_metrics: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    product_id: str = "",
    weights: Optional[Dict[str, int]] = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Score on a 0-100 scale. `weights` defaults to DEFAULT_WEIGHTS."""
    effective = weights if weights is not None else DEFAULT_WEIGHTS
    # ... rest unchanged but swap WEIGHTS[name] → effective[name] ...
```

Caller (`ai_spot_opinion.py::evaluate`) resolves user weights and passes through:

```python
if params.speculative_mode and not is_sell_check:
    from app.indicators.speculative_signals import (
        score_speculative_setup, summarize_components_for_prompt,
    )
    from app.services.speculative_weights_cache import get_effective_weights
    user_weights = await get_effective_weights(db, user_id)
    spec_score_result = score_speculative_setup(
        metrics, None, product_id, weights=user_weights,
    )
    spec_score_block = summarize_components_for_prompt(spec_score_result)
```

### 5. Monitor Extension

```python
# speculative_calibration_monitor.py
PROPOSAL_MIN_SAMPLE_SIZE = 500   # import from tuner to keep one source of truth

async def _check_user(db, user_id: int) -> None:
    # ... existing cooldown + analyze logic ...

    # NEW: Optionally create a proposal if the sample threshold is also crossed.
    proposal = None
    apply_url: Optional[str] = None
    if analysis["total_closed"] >= PROPOSAL_MIN_SAMPLE_SIZE:
        try:
            proposal = await _create_proposal(db, user_id, account.id, analysis)
            if proposal is not None:
                apply_url = build_apply_proposal_url(
                    user_id=user_id, account_id=account.id,
                    proposal_id=proposal.id, base_url=base_url,
                )
        except Exception:
            logger.exception(
                "speculative_calibration_monitor: proposal creation failed for user %s "
                "— alert still fires without an auto-proposal", user_id,
            )

    # Send email with optional proposal + apply_url
    email_ok = send_speculative_calibration_email(
        to=user.email, analysis=analysis,
        user_first_name=first_name, user_id=user_id,
        dismiss_url=dismiss_url,
        proposal=proposal,          # NEW — None means "don't render the proposal section"
        apply_url=apply_url,        # NEW
    )
    # ... rest unchanged ...
```

Proposal creation helper (same file or tuner module):

```python
async def _create_proposal(db, user_id, account_id, analysis) -> Optional[SpeculativeWeightsProposal]:
    """Run the tuner and persist a pending proposal. Returns the row, or None
    if the algorithm refuses (impossible constraints)."""
    current = await get_effective_weights(db, user_id)
    try:
        proposed = propose_weights(
            current_weights=current,
            component_stats=analysis["components"],
            overall_win_rate_pct=analysis["overall_win_rate_pct"],
        )
    except ValueError as exc:
        logger.warning("tuner refused to propose for user %s: %s", user_id, exc)
        return None

    if proposed == current:
        # No meaningful change — skip the proposal to avoid inbox noise.
        return None

    # Before inserting, mark any prior pending proposals as superseded.
    stmt = (
        update(SpeculativeWeightsProposal)
        .where(
            SpeculativeWeightsProposal.user_id == user_id,
            SpeculativeWeightsProposal.status == "pending",
        )
        .values(status="superseded", decided_at=datetime.utcnow())
    )
    await db.execute(stmt)

    row = SpeculativeWeightsProposal(
        user_id=user_id, account_id=account_id,
        status="pending",
        algorithm=ALGORITHM_NAME,
        sample_size=analysis["total_closed"],
        overall_win_rate_pct=analysis["overall_win_rate_pct"],
        baseline_weights=current,
        proposed_weights=proposed,
        divergence_pp=analysis.get("divergence_pp"),
        reason=_format_reason(analysis),
    )
    db.add(row)
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(row)
    return row
```

### 6. Email Extension

Signature change:
```python
def send_speculative_calibration_email(
    *, to, analysis, user_first_name, user_id, dismiss_url,
    proposal: Optional[SpeculativeWeightsProposal] = None,  # NEW
    apply_url: Optional[str] = None,                        # NEW
) -> bool:
```

New helper:
```python
def _render_proposal_block(proposal, apply_url) -> str:
    """Return the 'AUTOMATED PROPOSAL' section, or '' if no proposal."""
    if proposal is None or apply_url is None:
        return ""
    lines = [
        "━" * 44,
        f"AUTOMATED PROPOSAL (algorithm: {proposal.algorithm})",
        "━" * 44,
        "",
        "Based on your outcome data, the weight tuner suggests:",
        "",
        f"  {'Component':<24} {'Current':>8} → {'Proposed':>8}   {'(Δ)':>6}",
    ]
    for name, old in proposal.baseline_weights.items():
        new_w = proposal.proposed_weights.get(name, old)
        delta = new_w - old
        lines.append(f"  {name:<24} {old:>8} → {new_w:>8}   ({delta:+d})")
    lines += [
        "",
        "One-click apply (no Claude conversation required):",
        f"  {apply_url}",
        "",
        "Or, if you want to reason about these with Claude first, use",
        "the block below instead. Either way your current weights stay",
        "the same until you explicitly apply a change.",
        "",
    ]
    return "\n".join(lines)
```

Insert the block into `build_speculative_calibration_text_body()` between the existing components table and the "TO ACT ON THIS" Claude section. When `proposal is None`, the helper returns `""` and the email body is byte-identical to today's.

### 7. Apply-Proposal Token + URL

New module `app/services/speculative_calibration_apply_token.py` (separate file — crisp type boundary):

```python
_TOKEN_TYPE = "speculative_calibration_apply_proposal"
APPLY_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS = 30

def create_apply_proposal_token(*, user_id, account_id, proposal_id) -> str:
    payload = {
        "sub": str(user_id), "account_id": int(account_id),
        "proposal_id": int(proposal_id), "type": _TOKEN_TYPE,
        "exp": datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=APPLY_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS),
        "iat": datetime.utcnow(),
    }
    return jwt.encode(payload, settings.jwt_secret_key, algorithm=settings.jwt_algorithm)

def decode_apply_proposal_token(token: str) -> Optional[dict]:
    # Same structure as decode_dismiss_token, checks type == _TOKEN_TYPE.

def build_apply_proposal_url(*, user_id, account_id, proposal_id, base_url) -> str:
    token = create_apply_proposal_token(user_id=user_id, account_id=account_id, proposal_id=proposal_id)
    base = base_url.rstrip("/")
    return (
        f"{base}/settings"
        f"?apply_token={quote(token, safe='')}"
        f"&account_id={int(account_id)}"
        f"&proposal_id={int(proposal_id)}"
    )
```

### 8. Apply Endpoint

`POST /api/accounts/{account_id}/speculative-weights/apply-proposal` in `accounts_mutation_router.py`:

```python
@router.post("/{account_id}/speculative-weights/apply-proposal")
async def apply_speculative_weights_proposal(
    account_id: int,
    apply_token: str = Query(...),
    proposal_id: int = Query(...),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: User = Depends(require_permission(Perm.ACCOUNTS_WRITE)),
):
    # 1. Ownership check — same 404-on-foreign-account pattern as dismiss.
    account = (await db.execute(
        select(Account).where(Account.id == account_id, Account.user_id == current_user.id)
    )).scalar_one_or_none()
    if not account:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Not found")

    # 2. Token validation.
    from app.services.speculative_calibration_apply_token import decode_apply_proposal_token
    payload = decode_apply_proposal_token(apply_token)
    if not payload:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid apply token")
    if str(payload.get("sub")) != str(current_user.id):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid apply token")
    if int(payload.get("account_id") or 0) != int(account_id):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid apply token")
    if int(payload.get("proposal_id") or 0) != int(proposal_id):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid apply token")

    # 3. Load proposal, verify state.
    proposal = await db.get(SpeculativeWeightsProposal, proposal_id)
    if proposal is None or proposal.user_id != current_user.id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Proposal not found")
    if proposal.status != "pending":
        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f"Proposal is {proposal.status}, not pending")

    # 4. Apply.
    proposal.status = "applied"
    proposal.decided_at = datetime.utcnow()
    proposal.decided_by = current_user.id
    await db.commit()

    # 5. Invalidate the cache so the next scorer call picks up new weights.
    from app.services.speculative_weights_cache import invalidate_weights_cache
    invalidate_weights_cache(current_user.id)

    return {"applied": True, "weights": proposal.proposed_weights, "proposal_id": proposal_id}
```

### 9. Frontend Surface (minimal in Phase 1)

Two pieces on the Settings → Speculative Bucket section:

**a. Apply-link handler** (mirrors existing dismiss handler in `Settings.tsx`): on mount, if `?apply_token=&proposal_id=&account_id=` is present, POST to the apply endpoint, show success/error toast, strip params.

**b. Proposal history list** (new small component `SpeculativeWeightsHistory.tsx`): fetches `GET /api/accounts/{id}/speculative-weights/proposals` (also added to backend — simple read), renders a list of past proposals with their status + applied weights diff. Hidden if empty. This gives users visibility into "what changed when" without needing to pull DB logs.

The toast handler pattern is already established; the history component is ~80 lines of read-only table. Frontend tests follow the SpeculativeAllocationSection.test.tsx pattern.

## Implementation Tasks (in order)

### Task 1 — DB table + model (foundation)

## Algorithm Unit Test Blueprint (Task 2 — the highest-risk piece)

```python
# test_speculative_weights_tuner.py

DEFAULTS = {
    "volume_surge": 25, "compression_breakout": 20,
    "momentum_accelerating": 20, "micro_mid_cap": 10,
    "correlation_break": 10, "volume_vs_mcap": 15,
}

class TestPropose:
    def test_all_components_equal_win_rate_returns_defaults_unchanged(self):
        stats = [{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0} for k in DEFAULTS]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        assert result == DEFAULTS

    def test_above_baseline_component_gains_weight(self):
        stats = [
            {"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 30.0},
            # rest at baseline
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k != "volume_surge"],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        assert result["volume_surge"] > DEFAULTS["volume_surge"]

    def test_below_baseline_component_loses_weight(self):
        stats = [
            {"name": "correlation_break", "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 3.0},
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k != "correlation_break"],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        assert result["correlation_break"] < DEFAULTS["correlation_break"]

    def test_output_sums_to_100(self):
        stats = [
            {"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 30.0},
            {"name": "correlation_break", "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 3.0},
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k not in ("volume_surge", "correlation_break")],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        assert sum(result.values()) == 100

    def test_per_cycle_change_clamped_to_plus_minus_5(self):
        # Extreme divergence would otherwise push volume_surge far above +5.
        stats = [
            {"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 1000, "win_rate_pct": 95.0},
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 10.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k != "volume_surge"],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=10.0)
        assert abs(result["volume_surge"] - DEFAULTS["volume_surge"]) <= 5

    def test_floor_respected(self):
        # Extreme underperformance on a component that would otherwise drop below 5.
        stats = [
            {"name": "correlation_break", "fires": 1000, "win_rate_pct": 0.1},
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 50.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k != "correlation_break"],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=50.0)
        assert result["correlation_break"] >= 5

    def test_ceiling_respected(self):
        # volume_surge starting at 25 + clamp +5 = 30; but with an extreme sweep
        # where only volume_surge fires, the normalizer might try to push higher.
        # Ensure no value exceeds 40.
        stats = [{"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 1000, "win_rate_pct": 99.0}]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=10.0)
        assert all(v <= 40 for v in result.values())

    def test_zero_fires_component_keeps_current_weight(self):
        stats = [
            {"name": "volume_surge", "fires": 0, "win_rate_pct": 0.0},
            *[{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0}
              for k in DEFAULTS if k != "volume_surge"],
        ]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        # volume_surge is untouched (count as "no data, no opinion").
        assert result["volume_surge"] == DEFAULTS["volume_surge"]

    def test_rejects_impossible_constraints(self):
        # 6 components × floor=5 sums to 30; if we set target=10 it's impossible.
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            _normalize_to_sum(
                {k: 5.0 for k in DEFAULTS},
                target=10.0, floor=5.0, ceiling=40.0,
            )

    def test_integer_rounding_preserves_sum(self):
        # Specifically craft a scenario that produces fractional weights.
        weights = {"a": 33.3333, "b": 33.3333, "c": 33.3334}
        result = _integer_round_preserving_sum(weights, target=100)
        assert sum(result.values()) == 100

    def test_all_weights_integer_typed(self):
        stats = [{"name": k, "fires": 50, "win_rate_pct": 15.0 + i}
                 for i, k in enumerate(DEFAULTS)]
        result = propose_weights(DEFAULTS, stats, overall_win_rate_pct=15.0)
        assert all(isinstance(v, int) for v in result.values())
```

Success criteria (enforced by the tests):

- All unit tests green.
- Migration test asserts table + both indexes idempotently.
- Apply-endpoint test covers happy path + 403 on each token-mismatch axis (sub/account/proposal) + 409 on non-pending state.
- E2E test seeds 500+ outcomes, runs monitor, asserts proposal row, calls apply endpoint with the token extracted from the email body, asserts cache invalidation.
- Algorithm tests assert sum=100, floor=5, ceiling=40, ±5-change cap, and integer-rounding preservation.

---

## Gotchas & Pitfalls

1. **FK schema qualification**: Every `ForeignKey("X")` on the new model MUST be schema-qualified (`"trading.speculative_weights_proposals.id"` for the self-reference, `"auth.users.id"`, `"trading.accounts.id"`). Per MEMORY.md §SQLAlchemy schema, unqualified strings raise `NoReferencedTableError` on mapper config. SQLite tests won't catch this — production will.

2. **JSON column round-trip**: PostgreSQL JSONB returns a dict; SQLite `TEXT` column via SQLAlchemy's `JSON` type also returns dict in most cases but string in some. `_parse_weights()` tolerates both. Testing in SQLite will MOST of the time pass dicts, but include an explicit test that exercises both branches of `_parse_weights`.

3. **Normalizer convergence edge case**: if all components happen to need clamping to the same boundary, the algorithm can get stuck in the "no free components" state. The loop bound `range(len(w) + 1)` guarantees termination; the raise at the end is reachable only when constraints are truly unsatisfiable. Test this with `test_rejects_impossible_constraints`.

4. **Integer rounding sum drift**: `round()` in Python is banker's rounding. A naive `{k: round(v) for k, v in w.items()}` on 6 values can drift ±3 from 100. The largest-remainder method in `_integer_round_preserving_sum()` guarantees exact sum. Test explicitly.

5. **Cache coherence across requests**: The 60-second TTL means immediately after an apply, a stale request on another event-loop task can see the old weights for up to 60s. This is acceptable for scoring (next AI eval fetches fresh). But the apply-endpoint MUST also invalidate synchronously before returning 200, so the caller's NEXT request always sees the new weights.

6. **Supersede-before-insert race**: The UPDATE that marks prior pending proposals `superseded` must run in the same DB session as the INSERT of the new pending. Otherwise a concurrent monitor pass could insert a second pending before the first one's `superseded` flip. For the single-process monitor this is a non-issue; for future multi-worker setups add an advisory lock or unique partial index `(user_id) WHERE status='pending'`.

7. **Baseline-weights snapshot correctness**: `baseline_weights` on a new proposal MUST be the weights that are CURRENTLY effective for that user (which could be default OR a prior applied proposal), NOT `DEFAULT_WEIGHTS`. Resolve via `get_effective_weights(db, user_id)` before creating the proposal row. The e2e test verifies this.

8. **Email rendering with Unicode**: The box-drawing characters (`━`) in the proposal block match the existing header format. Ensure the email is sent with UTF-8 charset — it already is in `_send_email()`.

9. **Don't log the apply token**: JWTs in server logs = takeover surface. The monitor's `logger.info` about proposal creation should log `proposal_id` but not the URL.

10. **`decided_by` value**: Set to `current_user.id`, not `proposal.user_id`. These are always equal today (owner-only endpoint) but keeping the distinction in the audit table makes Phase 2's admin-assisted-apply story cleaner if we ever want it.

---

## Rollout

Single `/shipit` — Phase 1 is self-contained. Migration 084 is backwards-compatible (existing users have no row → scorer falls back to module defaults → behavior unchanged).

Post-ship: watch a handful of real speculative users accumulate past 50 positions (no-proposal alerts fire, functional today) and past 500 positions (proposal-mode kicks in, new behavior). No users are at 500 closed speculative positions today (feature is days old), so the actual proposal-generation code path won't activate in production immediately. That's a feature: the full pipeline is in place and tested end-to-end; it just won't fire until real data arrives.

Phase 2 is a separate PRP when this one has produced enough real proposals to validate the algorithm's suggestions.

---

## Outstanding Questions (for the USER to decide, not me)

1. **Threshold of 500 for auto-proposal**: is that the right number, or do you want to start looser (say 200) to exercise the path sooner? The alert itself still fires at 50 — this is just the auto-proposal gate.
2. **Learning rate of k=0.3**: conservative. Higher k (e.g. 0.5) means more aggressive re-tuning per cycle. Lower (0.15) means more stable but slower adaptation. 0.3 is a reasonable default but worth picking deliberately.
3. **Superseded-on-new-pending**: when a new proposal fires while an old one is still pending, should we auto-supersede the old one (current plan) or leave both pending? I lean auto-supersede to avoid confusing emails. Flag if you disagree.

Default to the plan above unless you override any of these before Task 6.
